question: [File: 'File Name: rest/service/tokenAllowanceService.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a spender.id filter with operator lte (less-than-or-equal) pointing to a very high spender ID, causing getSubQuery() to generate a WHERE clause that matches a large fraction of the token_allowance table for a given owner, forcing a large index range scan and increasing database IO and node resource consumption by over 30%?
